title: Annual Statement — Tax Year Summary & Email to Landlord

content.intro: The annual statement in RentGoose provides a full UK tax year summary for a landlord, covering all income, deductions, and VAT. It can be emailed directly to the landlord from within PropertyGoose.

sections.title: How to Generate and Send an Annual Statement

steps.title: Go to RentGoose Landlords
steps.content: Open RentGoose from the sidebar and click the Landlords tab.

steps.title: Open the Landlord's Financial View
steps.content: Find the landlord and click View to open their financial summary.

steps.title: Click Annual Statement
steps.content: Click the 'Annual Statement' button. A modal window opens showing the full tax year summary.

steps.title: Review the Summary
steps.content: The annual statement modal displays: gross rental income for the tax year, management fee deductions, other deductions (setup fees, repairs, etc.), VAT breakdown (if applicable), and net income paid to the landlord.

steps.title: Click Email to Landlord
steps.content: Click 'Email to Landlord' to send the statement directly to the landlord's email address held on their record in PropertyGoose.

steps.title: Landlord Receives Formatted Statement
steps.content: The landlord receives a formatted HTML email containing the full tax year statement. They can pass this to their accountant for self-assessment purposes.

sections.title: UK Tax Year Coverage
sections.content: Annual statements cover the UK tax year: 6 April to 5 April. Landlords should pass the statement to their accountant or tax advisor for self-assessment. The statement is suitable for use as supporting evidence of rental income.

sections.title: For Reference Only
sections.content: The statement includes a note that PropertyGoose figures are for reference based on records held in the system. Landlords should confirm all figures with a qualified accountant before submitting a tax return.
